page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6e.large is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, paired with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It features a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512F and AVX-512_VNNI. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.c6e.large in the lower performance tiers. Its stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1305.19 is categorized as weak, while the multi-core score of 1737.28 falls into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block transfers registering at 12.97 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.91 GB/sec. However, single-threaded bzip3 decompression shows average performance at 8.89 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are relatively low, reflecting its entry-level compute profile.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but modest resource footprint, the ecs.c6e.large offers a low-cost entry point for workloads requiring dedicated execution threads without high memory demands. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a poor fit for data-intensive or accelerated computing tasks. It is best suited for lightweight, compute-bound tasks such as basic web serving, simple utility scripting, single-threaded decompression tasks, or small-scale microservices that do not require high memory density.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6e.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, a 35 MB L3 cache, and supports AVX-512 instruction sets. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate modest performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores falling in the bottom 10%, though single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves average tier results. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance provides a cost-efficient entry point for lightweight, compute-bound workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, microservices, and simple utility scripts that do not require high memory or storage performance.

tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-optimized virtual instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a compact memory footprint for lightweight workloads.
